title: 「be about to + 動詞」句型
titleZh: 「be about to + 動詞」表示「正準備／即將」
titleEn: Using be about to for an imminent action

source.file: Sentence Structure 64 -「be about to + 動詞」表示「正準備／即將」.pdf
omissions: English versions of Chinese-only Important Rules and Benefits explanations are faithful editorial translations.

formula: Subject + am / is / are + about to + base verb

formulas.formula: Subject + am / is / are + about to + base verb
Subject + was / were + about to + base verb
As / When + subject + was / were about to + base verb, + past event
Subject + was / were about to + base verb + when + past event

example: The lesson is about to begin.

examples.en: The lesson is about to begin.
As John was about to leave, I asked for his phone number.
examples.zh: John 正準備離開時，我向他要了電話號碼。
examples.highlight: As John was about to leave

meaning.zh: Be about to 表示某個動作已非常接近發生，通常會在幾秒、幾分鐘或很短時間內發生。

rules.en: Use the base form of the verb after about to.
rules.zh: About to 後面使用動詞原形。

rules.en: Make the verb be agree with the subject and tense.
rules.zh: Be 動詞要配合主語和時態：現在式使用 am、is 或 are；過去式使用 was 或 were。

rules.en: Be about to normally describes something that will happen in seconds, minutes or a very short time.
rules.zh: Be about to 通常表示事情會在幾秒、幾分鐘或很短時間內發生；較遙遠的計劃通常使用 be going to 或其他將來式。
examples: The train is about to leave.

rules.en: Be going to can describe a later plan, whereas be about to places the event in the immediate future.
rules.zh: Be going to 可以表示較後時間的計劃；be about to 表示事情就在眼前，很快便會發生。
examples: We are going to move house next year.
We are about to leave the house.

rules.en: Was about to shows that an event came very close to happening, but does not prove that it was completed.
rules.zh: Was about to 說明事情已非常接近發生，但不一定真的完成；另一件事情可能在最後一刻打斷它。
examples: The train was about to leave when the driver saw a problem.

rules.en: This lesson does not use not about to to express refusal or unwillingness.
rules.zh: 較高階英語可用 not about to 表示拒絕或不願意，但這並非本課所學的「事情即將發生」，所以練習不包括這種意思。
examples: I am not about to apologise.

benefits.en: It shows more precisely than an ordinary future form that something will happen very soon.
benefits.zh: 這個句型比普通的將來式更準確，能表明事情已經非常接近發生。

benefits.en: It naturally describes an action interrupted at the last moment.
benefits.zh: 它能清楚交代原本快要發生的事情、突然出現的另一件事情，以及兩者的先後關係。

benefits.en: It can replace a longer explanation containing in a few seconds, in a moment or very soon.
benefits.zh: 不需要每次都使用 in a few seconds、in a moment 或 very soon，也可以直接表達事情即將發生。

benefits.en: It makes stories more vivid and immediate.
benefits.zh: 讀者能想像事情已準備發生卻在最後一刻被打斷，使故事更緊湊和有畫面。

benefits.en: The frame I was about to... when... helps you speak fluently about sudden events.
benefits.zh: 說故事或描述突發情況時，可以使用 I was about to... when... 的框架迅速加入自己的內容。

sourceOmissions: English versions of Chinese-only Important Rules and Benefits explanations are faithful editorial translations.

en: Rewrite each sentence using be about to + base verb to show that an action is going to happen very soon.
Use as or when where necessary to connect an action with another event that happened at that moment.
The first word of each answer has been provided.
zh: 使用 be about to + 動詞原形 改寫以下句子，以表示某個動作正準備或即將發生。
如有需要，使用 as 或 when，把正準備發生的動作與當時出現的另一件事情連接起來。
每題已提供答案的第一個字。

questions.prompt: I will start my homework in a moment.
questions.promptZh: 我很快便會開始做家課。
questions.answer: I am about to start my homework.
questions.answerZh: 我正準備開始做家課。
questions.highlight: I am about to start my homework.

questions.promptZh: 米婭會在幾秒後打開門。
